beats.narration_text: Let's recap with Claude. The Feb 13 snapshot maps H1B sponsorship behavior across employers and industries. Analysis links industry and funding stage to the likelihood of first-time sponsorship. Forty-seven identified companies have never sponsored an H1B visa before. Their scraped websites become inputs for predicting future sponsorship likelihood.

artifactLines: The Feb 13 snapshot maps H1B sponsorship behavior across employers and industries.
Analysis links industry and funding stage to the likelihood of first-time sponsorship.
Forty-seven identified companies have never sponsored an H1B visa before.
Their scraped websites become inputs for predicting future sponsorship likelihood.

beats.narration_text: The February 13 snapshot pointing to 47 companies that have never sponsored an H1B before really grabs me, especially the idea of predicting first-time sponsorship from firmographics. I want to build a starter model from the scraped websites. Guide me through: (1) which firmographic features (industry, funding stage, headcount) most likely correlate with first-time sponsorship; (2) how to label the 47 never-sponsored companies as a target class; (3) what a simple classifier's precision would need to be to be useful to a job seeker.
